title: Dentist

rationales.taskAutomation: Routine restorative and preventive procedures require licensed hands-on dexterity; only imaging review and treatment-plan documentation carry a meaningful digital share.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI caries-detection and imaging-analysis tools (Overjet, Pearl) are credible and improving, not yet standard equipment in most practices.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is concentrated in larger dental service organizations; independent practices are mostly early or non-adopters.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-screen bitewing X-rays and draft treatment-plan documentation, but the procedures themselves stay physically hands-on.

tasksAtRisk: AI-assisted caries and pathology detection on X-rays
tasksGrowing: Complex case diagnosis and treatment sequencing
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1021 (Dentists, General), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now flags caries and bone loss on X-rays before the dentist even looks, tightening diagnosis but not touching the drill. The procedures that define the job stay licensed, physical, and human for the foreseeable future.
